description: Native Nigerian English. Female, early 50s. Clear, well-supported. Persona: Dignified household keeper. Emotion: warm, formal, exacting. A low, definite alto with crisp consonants and a Nigerian English lilt sitting under Detroit vowels. Brisk, efficient pacing with deliberate emphasis on what is owed, each sentence closing exactly where she means it.
text: They're from the Vesely place... the brown ones. [deliberate] She said you'd want the brown ones, and she was right. We pay what we owe, in advance, before the visit Tuesday. [warm] A debt kept clean keeps a house standing.
